verses.text: Against Moab, who 'burned the bones of the king of Edom into lime'; fire shall devour Moab. Vengeful spite. Even cruelty to a dead enemy is marked by God.
Against Moab, who 'burned the bones of the king of Edom into lime'; fire shall devour Moab. Vengeful spite. Even cruelty to a dead enemy is marked by God.
Against Moab, who 'burned the bones of the king of Edom into lime'; fire shall devour Moab. Vengeful spite. Even cruelty to a dead enemy is marked by God.
Against Judah, who 'despised the law of the LORD' and followed lies; fire shall consume her. God's own judged. Privilege does not exempt; despising His law brings judgment.
Against Judah, who 'despised the law of the LORD' and followed lies; fire shall consume her. God's own judged. Privilege does not exempt; despising His law brings judgment.
Against Israel, who 'sold the righteous for silver, and the poor for a pair of shoes,' and oppress the meek. Injustice exposed. God hears the cry of the poor sold for trifles.
Against Israel, who 'sold the righteous for silver, and the poor for a pair of shoes,' and oppress the meek. Injustice exposed. God hears the cry of the poor sold for trifles.
Against Israel, who 'sold the righteous for silver, and the poor for a pair of shoes,' and oppress the meek. Injustice exposed. God hears the cry of the poor sold for trifles.
Yet He destroyed the Amorite before them, brought them from Egypt, and raised up prophets and Nazarites. Mercies recalled. Their sin is the blacker against such kindness.
Yet He destroyed the Amorite before them, brought them from Egypt, and raised up prophets and Nazarites. Mercies recalled. Their sin is the blacker against such kindness.
Yet He destroyed the Amorite before them, brought them from Egypt, and raised up prophets and Nazarites. Mercies recalled. Their sin is the blacker against such kindness.
But they corrupted the Nazarites with wine and silenced the prophets; God is 'pressed' under them as a loaded cart. Grace abused. To stifle God's word and witnesses provokes Him sorely.
But they corrupted the Nazarites with wine and silenced the prophets; God is 'pressed' under them as a loaded cart. Grace abused. To stifle God's word and witnesses provokes Him sorely.
In that day the swift shall not flee, the strong not save himself; the brave 'shall flee away naked.' No escape. Human strength is nothing in the day of God's reckoning.
